items.title: Never Sacrifice Public Health For Revenue — Malaysian Pharmacists Society
items.summary: The Court’s findings are also an important reminder that process matters. Expert advice matters. Consultation cannot become a procedural exercise after decisions have effectively already been made. The Poisons Board is not a decorative committee. The post Never Sacrifice Public Health For Revenue — Malaysian Pharmacists Society appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Analysis: Court Decision Marks De Facto Ban On Nicotine Vape
items.summary: The court ruling means that liquid nicotine stays in the Poisons List because it was never (legally) removed in the first place. Hence there's now a de facto ban on nicotine vape: sales and tax collection became illegal instantly upon the court's decision. The post Analysis: Court Decision Marks De Facto Ban On Nicotine Vape appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Authorities Must Take Immediate Action After Court Ruling On Liquid Nicotine — MCTC
items.summary: MCTC hopes that this court decision will serve as an important reminder that all public health policies must be formulated transparently, with integrity, free from industry influence, and in line with Malaysia’s obligations under WHO FCTC and the UN CRC. The post Authorities Must Take Immediate Action After Court Ruling On Liquid Nicotine — MCTC appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Court Ruling On Illegal Nicotine Delisting A Huge Victory For Public Health
items.summary: Today's High Court decision to strike down the delisting of liquid nicotine is a significant victory for public health. It puts the proverbial genie back in the bottle and renders the sale and collection of taxes on nicotine vape mostly illegal. The post Court Ruling On Illegal Nicotine Delisting A Huge Victory For Public Health appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: ProtectHealth Says Not Yet Hit By Spending Cuts
items.summary: ProtectHealth Corp says MOH has not ordered any scale down of its schemes despite proposed federal budget cuts. It also says bulk purchasing has helped contain costs, while the Base MHIT and DRG remain on track despite Iran war pressures. The post ProtectHealth Says Not Yet Hit By Spending Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: High Court Rules Liquid Nicotine Was Delisted Unlawfully
items.summary: In a stunning court ruling, the High Court decided that Dr Zaliha Mustafa acted illegally in removing liquid nicotine from the Poisons List, saying the delisting was mainly based on economic reasons and the health minister didn't consult the Poisons Board. The post High Court Rules Liquid Nicotine Was Delisted Unlawfully appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Opaque Selection In MOH For Transfers, Master’s Programme — Medical Officer
items.summary: The way selection and transfer processes are handled for government doctors reflects a culture of opacity and disregard for basic professional decency. It's hard to retain health workers when the system treats their time and personal lives as disposable. The post Opaque Selection In MOH For Transfers, Master’s Programme — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Don’t Victimise Health Workers With Salary Cuts: Hartal
items.summary: Hartal Doktor Kontrak says the government shouldn't be talking about salary cuts for civil servants, even as an “absolute last resort”, that would end up demoralising the public service. "We trust that PMX won't resort to victimising health care workers." The post Don’t Victimise Health Workers With Salary Cuts: Hartal appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH Maintains Status Quo After UK Royal College Dubs Parallel Pathway ‘Malaysian Qualification’
items.summary: MOH says there's no need to amend Schedule 4 of the Medical Act, perceiving RCSEd's statement that the cardiothoracic surgery parallel pathway is a "Malaysian qualification" as referring to "implementation" of the programme, not a change in conferment. The post MOH Maintains Status Quo After UK Royal College Dubs Parallel Pathway ‘Malaysian Qualification’ appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Form National Health Authority For Workforce, Service Delivery — Chua Hong Teck
items.summary: Malaysia should form a National Health Authority as a statutory operator of public health care services responsible for workforce management, in a devolution of these roles from MOH that can focus solely on national public health, policy, and regulation. The post Form National Health Authority For Workforce, Service Delivery — Chua Hong Teck appeared first on CodeBlue .
